Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single product available for sale in the e-commerce store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product, potentially AI-generated.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the product, derived from its name.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

brand.description: The brand name of the product.

isPublished.description: Indicates whether the product is visible and available for purchase on the storefront.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product was last updated.

ProductVariant.title: ProductVariant
ProductVariant.description: Represents a specific variation of a product (e.g., a specific size and color of a shirt).

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductVariant entity.

productId.description: Reference to the parent Product. (Relationship: Product 1:N ProductVariant)

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit for this specific variant.

price.description: The selling price of this variant. Overrides product's base price if specified.

compareAtPrice.description: An optional original price for comparison, indicating a discount for this variant.

options.description: A list of key-value pairs describing the variant's attributes (e.g., [{'name': 'Color', 'value': 'Red'}, {'name': 'Size', 'value': 'M'}]).

inventoryId.description: Reference to the Inventory record for this variant. (Relationship: ProductVariant 1:1 Inventory)

ProductImage.title: ProductImage
ProductImage.description: Stores image information for products and their variants.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductImage entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product this image belongs to. (Relationship: Product 1:N ProductImage)

variantId.description: Optional reference to a specific ProductVariant if the image is variant-specific. (Relationship: ProductVariant 1:N ProductImage)

url.description: The URL of the product image.

altText.description: Alternative text for the image, for accessibility and SEO.

sortOrder.description: The display order of the image relative to other images for the same product/variant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the category.

parentCategoryId.description: Optional reference to a parent category for hierarchical categorization. (Relationship: Category 1:N Category - self-referencing)

sizeChartId.description: Reference to the SizeChart associated with this category. (Relationship: SizeChart 1:N Category)

imageUrl.description: An optional image URL representing the category.

SizeChart.title: SizeChart
SizeChart.description: Provides category-specific sizing information for products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SizeChart entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the size chart (e.g., 'Men\'s T-Shirt Sizes').

description.description: A brief description of what this size chart covers.

data.description: An array of objects, where each object represents a row of measurement data (e.g., [{'measurement': 'Chest', 'unit': 'in', 'S': '34-36', 'M': '38-40'}]).

Collection.title: Collection
Collection.description: Groups products for marketing and display purposes, such as featured collections or new drops.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Collection entity.

name.description: The name of the collection (e.g., 'Summer Essentials', 'New Arrivals').

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the collection.

productIds.description: An array of Product IDs included in this collection. (Relationship: Product N:N Collection)

imageUrl.description: An optional image URL for displaying the collection (e.g., for hero sections).

sortOrder.description: The display order of the collection.

Inventory.title: Inventory
Inventory.description: Tracks the stock levels for product variants.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inventory entity.

productVariantId.description: Reference to the ProductVariant this inventory record is for. (Relationship: ProductVariant 1:1 Inventory)

quantity.description: The current stock quantity of the product variant.

reorderPoint.description: The quantity level at which a reorder should be triggered.

location.description: Optional physical location of the inventory (e.g., 'Warehouse A', 'Shelf 3').

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inventory record was last updated.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer of the store. Relies on an external authentication system for core user management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity, typically provided by an external authentication system.

defaultShippingAddressId.description: Reference to the customer's default shipping address. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Address)

defaultBillingAddressId.description: Reference to the customer's default billing address. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Address)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was last updated.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Stores a physical address associated with a customer or an order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer this address belongs to. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Address)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the street address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the street address (optional, e.g., apartment number).

stateProvince.description: The state or province of the address.

postalCode.description: The postal or zip code of the address.

isDefaultShipping.description: Indicates if this is the customer's default shipping address.

isDefaultBilling.description: Indicates if this is the customer's default billing address.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a completed or pending customer purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who placed the order. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Order)

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'processing', 'shipped', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

subtotal.description: The total cost of items before shipping and taxes.

shippingCost.description: The cost of shipping for this order.

taxCost.description: The total tax amount for this order.

total.description: The grand total amount for the order.

shippingAddressId.description: Reference to the shipping address used for this order. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

billingAddressId.description: Reference to the billing address used for this order. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

trackingNumber.description: The tracking number for the shipment.

orderNote.description: Any special notes or instructions provided by the customer for the order.

paymentStatus.description: The status of the payment for this order (e.g., 'paid', 'pending', 'refunded').

paymentIntentId.description: Reference ID from the payment gateway (e.g., Stripe Payment Intent ID).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order was last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents an individual item within a customer's order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the parent Order. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productVariantId.description: Reference to the ProductVariant purchased. (Relationship: ProductVariant 1:N OrderItem)

productName.description: Snapshot of the product name at the time of purchase.

quantity.description: The quantity of this product variant ordered.

priceAtPurchase.description: The unit price of the product variant at the time of purchase.

totalPrice.description: The total price for this order item (quantity * priceAtPurchase).

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a customer's shopping cart, persisting items across sessions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity, often corresponding to the customer ID or a session ID.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who owns this cart. (Relationship: Customer 1:1 Cart)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was last updated.

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents an individual item within a customer's shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the parent Cart. (Relationship: Cart 1:N CartItem)

productVariantId.description: Reference to the ProductVariant added to the cart. (Relationship: ProductVariant 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of this product variant in the cart.

addedAt.description: Timestamp when the item was added to the cart.

ShippingZone.title: ShippingZone
ShippingZone.description: Defines a geographical area for which specific shipping rates apply.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShippingZone entity.

name.description: The name of the shipping zone (e.g., 'Domestic', 'Europe').

countries.description: An array of country codes (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) included in this zone.

statesProvinces.description: An optional array of state or province codes included in this zone.

ShippingRate.title: ShippingRate
ShippingRate.description: Defines the cost and conditions for shipping within a specific shipping zone.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShippingRate entity.

shippingZoneId.description: Reference to the ShippingZone this rate applies to. (Relationship: ShippingZone 1:N ShippingRate)

name.description: The name of the shipping rate (e.g., 'Standard Shipping', 'Express').

type.description: The type of shipping rate calculation (e.g., 'flat', 'weight_based', 'price_based').

cost.description: The flat cost of shipping if type is 'flat'.

minWeight.description: The minimum weight for this rate to apply (for 'weight_based').

maxWeight.description: The maximum weight for this rate to apply (for 'weight_based').

costPerWeightUnit.description: The cost per unit of weight (e.g., per kg) for 'weight_based' rates.

minPrice.description: The minimum order subtotal for this rate to apply (for 'price_based').

maxPrice.description: The maximum order subtotal for this rate to apply (for 'price_based').

freeShippingThreshold.description: The minimum order subtotal for free shipping to apply with this rate.

PaymentGatewayConfig.title: PaymentGatewayConfig
PaymentGatewayConfig.description: Stores configuration details for integrated payment gateways like Stripe or PayPal.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentGatewayConfig entity (e.g., 'stripe', 'paypal').

name.description: The display name of the payment gateway (e.g., 'Stripe Credit Card', 'PayPal').

apiKey.description: The public API key for the payment gateway. Assumed to be securely stored/encrypted.

secretKey.description: The secret API key for the payment gateway. Assumed to be securely stored/encrypted.

isSandboxMode.description: Toggle to switch between sandbox (test) and live modes for the gateway.

isEnabled.description: Indicates if this payment gateway is active and available for use.

StoreSetting.title: StoreSetting
StoreSetting.description: Holds global configuration settings for the entire e-commerce store, including theme and layout.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StoreSetting entity (e.g., 'global' for the main settings document).

storeName.description: The official name of the e-commerce store.

logoUrl.description: URL to the store's logo image.

primaryColor.description: Hex code for the primary brand color (#000000).

backgroundColor.description: Hex code for the main background color (#F4F4F4).

accentColor.description: Hex code for the accent color (#FFFFFF).

headlineFont.description: The font family for headlines (e.g., 'Playfair Display').

bodyFont.description: The font family for body text (e.g., 'Inter').

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the store settings were last updated.

Banner.title: Banner
Banner.description: Manages promotional banners displayed on the storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Banner entity.

name.description: An internal name for the banner (e.g., 'Homepage Hero Banner', 'Free Shipping Promo').

text.description: The main text content displayed on the banner.

backgroundColor.description: Hex code for the banner's background color.

linkUrl.description: The URL the banner links to when clicked.

visibility.description: Controls whether the banner is currently visible on the storefront.

placement.description: Describes where on the site the banner should be displayed (e.g., 'home_hero', 'promo_bar').

sortOrder.description: The display order of the banner if multiple banners are in the same placement.

imageUrl.description: Optional image URL for the banner.

SocialSync.title: SocialSync
SocialSync.description: Manages integration and synchronization status with external social commerce platforms.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SocialSync entity (e.g., 'tiktok', 'instagram', 'google_shopping').

platform.description: The name of the social commerce platform (e.g., 'TikTok Shop', 'Instagram', 'Google Shopping').

apiKey.description: API key or token for integration with the platform. Assumed to be securely stored/encrypted.

lastSyncAt.description: Timestamp of the last successful synchronization with the platform.

webhookUrl.description: The URL to trigger a manual synchronization webhook for the platform.

isEnabled.description: Indicates if this social platform integration is active.

definition.description: Stores individual product details. Publicly readable for storefront display, admin-only write. All documents in this collection share the same security posture. Includes denormalized 'isPublished' field for explicit state modeling.
Stores specific variations of a product. Publicly readable, admin-only write. Inherits access posture from parent product. 'productId' is denormalized for direct reference.

params.name: productId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent Product.

definition.description: Stores image URLs and metadata for products and their variants. Publicly readable, admin-only write. 'productId' is denormalized for direct reference.

params.name: productId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent Product.

definition.description: Organizes products into logical groups. Publicly readable for storefront, admin-only write. All documents in this collection share the same security posture.
Provides category-specific sizing information. Publicly readable (as per user request regarding list permission), admin-only write. All documents in this collection share the same security posture.
Groups products for marketing and display. Publicly readable, admin-only write. All documents in this collection share the same security posture.

Tracks stock levels for product variants. Admin-only read/write due to sensitive nature. 'productVariantId' denormalized for direct reference. All documents in this collection share the same security posture.

params.name: inventoryId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Inventory, typically matching productVariantId due to 1:1 relationship.

definition.description: Stores customer profile data. Owner (userId) has read/write, admin has read access. The 'id' field matches the path's 'userId' for authorization independence. All documents in this collection share the same security posture.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Customer, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores physical addresses for a customer. Owner (userId) has read/write, admin has read access. Authorization is path-based, ensuring independence. 'customerId' is denormalized for data integrity.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent Customer.

definition.description: Represents a customer's purchase. Owner (userId) has read/write, admin has read/write access. Authorization is path-based. 'customerId', 'shippingAddressId', 'billingAddressId' are denormalized for context.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent Customer.

definition.description: Individual items within a customer's order. Owner (userId via parent order) has read/write, admin has read/write access. Authorization is path-based. 'orderId' and 'productVariantId' are denormalized.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier of the top-level Customer.

params.name: orderId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent Order.

definition.description: Represents a customer's shopping cart (single document per user). Owner (userId) has read/write, admin has read access. Authorization is path-based. 'customerId' is denormalized.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent Customer.

definition.description: Individual items within a customer's cart. Owner (userId via parent cart) has read/write, admin has read access. Authorization is path-based. 'cartId' and 'productVariantId' are denormalized.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier of the top-level Customer.

definition.description: Defines geographical areas for shipping. Publicly readable (for checkout calculations), admin-only write. All documents in this collection share the same security posture.
Defines cost and conditions for shipping within a zone. Publicly readable, admin-only write. Inherits access posture from parent shipping zone. 'shippingZoneId' is denormalized.

params.name: zoneId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent ShippingZone.

definition.description: Stores sensitive payment gateway configurations. Admin-only read/write. Assumes sensitive keys are handled securely (e.g., backend functions) and not directly exposed to clients. All documents in this collection share the same security posture.
Holds global configuration settings for the store, including theme and layout. Publicly readable for UI rendering, admin-only write. All documents in this collection share the same security posture.
Manages promotional banners. Publicly readable, admin-only write. All documents in this collection share the same security posture.

Manages integration status with social commerce platforms. Admin-only read/write. Assumes API keys are handled securely (e.g., backend functions) and not directly exposed to clients. All documents in this collection share the same security posture.

params.name: platformId
params.description: Unique identifier for the SocialSync platform (e.g., 'tiktok').

definition.description: Manages administrative roles based on Firebase Auth UIDs. Existence of a document signifies admin status (DBAC). Admin-only create/delete/update for other admins, self-read/delete. This collection enables Authorization Independence for admin checks.

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence and Query-Able Permissions (QAPs) to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence:**

1.  **Path-Based Ownership for User Data:** All user-specific data (Customer profiles, Addresses, Carts, Orders, and their respective items) are nested under `/users/{userId}/...`. This design ensures that the `userId` in the path directly corresponds to the authenticated user's `request.auth.uid`. This makes authorization highly independent; access to any document within this hierarchy can be determined solely by comparing the path's `userId` with `request.auth.uid` and doesn't require `get()` calls to a parent document's fields. The `customerId` field within these documents acts as a denormalized reference, reinforcing data integrity but not being strictly necessary for rule evaluation itself.
2.  **Explicit Denormalization in Subcollections:** For subcollections like `products/{productId}/variants` and `shippingZones/{zoneId}/rates`, while their primary authorization is inherited from the parent collection's general posture (public read/admin write), the child documents still explicitly include `productId` and `shippingZoneId` respectively. This maintains a strong referential link and could be used in more granular rules if needed, without relying on `get()` to the parent for basic access.
3.  **Global Admin Roles:** Administrative access is managed via the top-level collection `/roles_admin/{uid}`. The existence of a document for a specific `uid` in this collection grants admin privileges. This completely decouples admin authorization from any other data documents, making it atomic and independent.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

1.  **Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security Posture:** To enable secure `list` operations, data is segregated into collections with uniform security requirements. 
    *   **Public Read Collections:** Entities intended for public consumption (e.g., `products`, `categories`, `sizeCharts`, `collections`, `storeSettings`, `banners`, `shippingZones` and their subcollections) reside in top-level collections. All documents within these collections are publicly readable. This means a public `list` query on these collections will retrieve *only* data that is genuinely public, without needing complex security rules to filter out private items on the server side.
    *   **Admin-Only Collections:** Sensitive operational data like `inventory`, `paymentGatewayConfigs`, and `socialSyncs` are stored in dedicated top-level collections accessible only by administrators. This ensures that `list` operations on these collections only return admin-authorized data.
    *   **User-Private Collections:** Data belonging to individual users is strictly nested under `/users/{userId}/...`. A `list` operation on a collection like `/users/{userId}/orders` will inherently only return orders for the authenticated `userId`, thus fulfilling the QAP principle without server-side filtering by rules.

This architecture minimizes rule complexity, prevents security vulnerabilities arising from `get()` dependencies, and ensures efficient, predictable query behavior, especially for `list` operations.
